x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces PDC's reviewed-project / meeting / public-art-collection / commissioner / award data, and adds submit_design_review — the net-new B2G write workflow that today is a manual PDF application form emailed by a City agency liaison. There is no citizen write in this domain.

server.name: io.nyc.pdc
server.title: NYC Public Design Commission (PDC)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Public Design Commission — the City's design-review agency for permanent architecture, landscape architecture, and art on or over City-owned property (NYC Charter Chapter 37). Search projects reviewed by the Commission, monthly meeting agendas, the City's public art & monument collection, the 11 pro-bono commissioners, and the annual design awards, plus the net-new ability for a City agency to submit a project for design review that today is a manual PDF/email transmittal.
server.instructions: Use find_projects / get_project to locate projects the Commission has reviewed, by borough, project type (architecture/landscape/artwork/signage/…), level of review (conceptual/preliminary/final), action (consent vs. public hearing), result, or lead/secondary agency. NOTE the project data comes from periodic Open Data snapshots (Monthly Design Review + Annual Report), not a live feed. find_meetings / get_meeting cover the monthly meeting calendar and agendas (consent items batch-voted, public hearings fully reviewed) — these are reverse-engineered from the site because there is no Open Data twin. search_collection / get_collection_item cover the City's outdoor public art & monument collection PDC curates. list_commissioners lists the 11 pro-bono members. find_awards lists Awards for Excellence in Design recipients. submit_design_review (net-new) is an agency/B2G write — only a City agency with jurisdiction over the property may submit, it must be signed by all involved agencies and list Community Board(s) and Council District(s), and it mutates state; confirm the lead agency, category, and project details with the user before submitting. This is an agency-facing commission: there is no citizen service or citizen write here.

tools.name: find_projects
tools.title: Find Projects
tools.description: Search projects reviewed by the Public Design Commission by borough, project type, level of review, action (consent/public hearing), result, agency, year, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_project
tools.title: Get Project
tools.description: Fetch a reviewed project (title, type, level of review, action, result, construction type, agencies, meeting date) by PROJECT_ID.

tools.name: find_meetings
tools.title: Find Meetings
tools.description: List Public Design Commission meetings and their agendas by date range or kind (Committee Meeting, Public Meeting, Public Hearing). Reverse-engineered from the site — there is no Open Data twin for the calendar/agendas.

tools.name: get_meeting
tools.title: Get Meeting
tools.description: Fetch a single Commission meeting and its agenda items (consent items and public hearings) by id (date).

tools.name: search_collection
tools.title: Search Collection
tools.description: Search the City's public art & monument collection PDC curates (Outdoor Public Art Inventory) by borough, artwork type, material, artist, or managing agency.

tools.name: get_collection_item
tools.title: Get Collection Item
tools.description: Fetch a single work in the City's public art & monument collection (title, creators, material, location, inscription, dates) by title.

tools.name: list_commissioners
tools.title: List Commissioners
tools.description: List the 11 pro-bono members of the Public Design Commission and the statutory seats they fill (architect, landscape architect, painter, sculptor, lay members, museum/library/mayoral representatives).

tools.name: find_awards
tools.title: Find Awards
tools.description: List Awards for Excellence in Design recipients by year, cycle, or borough.

tools.name: list_my_submissions
tools.title: List My Submissions
tools.description: List the authenticated City agency's own design-review submissions and their status — today visible only inside PDC's manual intake.

tools.name: submit_design_review
tools.title: Submit Design Review
tools.description: Submit a project to the Public Design Commission for design review (architecture, landscape architecture, or art on/over City-owned property). NET-NEW workflow with no PDC-owned API today — submission is currently a manual PDF application form and checklist emailed by a City agency liaison, with hard copies delivered to City Hall. This is an agency/B2G transaction (no citizen write exists in this domain): only a City agency with jurisdiction over the property may submit, it must be signed by all involved agencies and list Community Board(s) and Council District(s). Mutates state — confirm lead agency, category, and project details first.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.

prompts.name: explore_pdc
prompts.description: Search and read NYC Public Design Commission (PDC) records — Projects, Meetings, Collection, Commissioners.

prompts.name: apply_for_pdc
prompts.description: DesignSubmission — a City agency (via its PDC liaison) submits a project for design review (category, requested level of review, lead + secondary agencies, Community Board / Council District, interagency approvals, and a

resources.name: NYC Public Design Commission (PDC) — profile
resources.description: NYC Public Design Commission (PDC): 11 operations, 10 tools, verb “digitize”.

resources.name: Projects record (templated)
resources.description: A single NYC Public Design Commission (PDC) Projects record by id.
